name: Wild Mushroom Risotto
recipeIngredient: 4 ounces Shiitake mushrooms (or other wild mushrooms, stems removed and sliced)
1/2 cup Dry white wine or broth
1/4 cup Mascarpone cheese (or whipping cream, or substitute skim milk)
recipeInstructions:
You can substitute dried wild mushrooms for the shiitake. Just soak them for 15-to-20 minutes beforehand (and be sure to add the strained soaking liquid to the stock).
Heat butter in a small skillet over moderate heat. When it begins to foam, add mushrooms and cook until soft, about 3-to-5 minutes. Add salt and pepper to taste.
Turn off heat and set aside.
Bring broth to a steady simmer in a saucepan on top of the stove.
Heat butter and oil in a heavy 4-quart casserole over moderate heat. Add onion and saute until it begins to soften, being careful not to brown.
Add rice to butter-oil-onion mixture. Stir with a wooden spoon for 1 minute, making sure all grains are well coated.
Add wine and stir until completely absorbed.
Add simmering broth, about 1/2 cup at a time, stirring frequently. Wait until each addition is almost completely absorbed (about 2 minutes) before adding the next 1/2 cup, reserving 1/4 cup to add at the end.
Stir often. After approximately 18 minutes (rice should be tender and firm), stir in reserved broth, mushrooms, Mascarpone cheese, Parmesan cheese, and parsley. Stir vigorously until cheeses melt.
Serve immediately.
